mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market slack that reduces corporate profit growth expectations, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and CPI inflation indicates demand-pull overheating that drives positive corporate earnings revisions and positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL yield negative 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of months with declining INDPRO during tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that boosts wage pressures and corporate margins, generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window or the signal yields negative returns in greater than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month reflects strong consumer spending that directly supports corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of months when RSAFS MoM exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL exceeding 245 signals entrenched inflation that forces central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds 245.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate leading demand strength in construction and related sectors, generating positive short-term equity returns as investors front-run earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of months with rising HOUST.
